chunks.text: Apple Mail | IRIS Skip to main content Skip to primary navigation Skip to primary sidebar Apple Mail This FAQ gives a step by step guideline on how to configure Apple Mail for your Berkeley bMail account. Enable IMAP in your Gmail settings How to cre

unt. Enable IMAP in your Gmail settings How to create a bConnected Key Quick Settings Creating A New Account Setting up LDAP Autocomplete Quick Settings Add your bMail to Apple Mail via Google: Launch the Mail app and select “Google” then click “Cont

the Mail app and select “Google” then click “Continue”. Enter your full @berkeley.edu email address then click “Next”. Enter your CalNet ID and Passphrase then click “Sign In”. Select the items you would like to synchronize (Mail, Calendar, Contacts

ould like to synchronize (Mail, Calendar, Contacts, etc.) then click “Done”. For those of you who already know how to set up Apple Mail but just need the server names and port numbers, they are: INCOMING SERVER: imap.gmail.com port 993 (use SSL) OUTG

ING SERVER: imap.gmail.com port 993 (use SSL) OUTGOING SERVER: smtp.gmail.com port 465 (use SSL) USERNAME: username@berkeley.edu (not just “username”) PASSWORD: your bConnected Key Creating A New Account You will first need to be sure that your bMail

unt You will first need to be sure that your bMail account has IMAP enabled, and that you have created a bConnected Key. bConnected Key Instructions (for older clients without 2-step authentication): After loading Mail, a startup screen should appear

After loading Mail, a startup screen should appear. Enter your full name, email address, and your bConnected Key: Select “IMAP” for Account Type; enter bMail for Description, imap.gmail.com for the Incoming Mail Server, and username@berkeley.edu for

coming Mail Server, and username@berkeley.edu for User Name (not just “username”). For the Outgoing Mail Server, enter bMail for Description, smtp.gmail.com for the server, and click “Use Authentication” (your User Name and Password should already be

on” (your User Name and Password should already be filled in). This last page gives you a summary of your information. If everything checks out, click Continue. Setting up LDAP Autocomplete The EECS LDAP servers restrict off-campus access, so to use

DAP servers restrict off-campus access, so to use this auto-completion while off campus, you will need to use the Campus VPN Service . Go to Mail -> Preferences -> Composing. Under Addressing, click on “Configure LDAP…”. In the next window, click the

n “Configure LDAP…”. In the next window, click the + to add a new server. Enter the Server Info: Name: EECS Server: ldap.eecs.berkeley.edu Click Use SSL (port 636 will be used) Search Base: ou=people,o=eecs.berkeley.edu,dc=eecs,dc=berkeley,dc=edu Cli

o=eecs.berkeley.edu,dc=eecs,dc=berkeley,dc=edu Click Save and Done. Primary Sidebar IRIS Service Status We have 0 Active Incidents, and 1 Scheduled Maintenance noted. IST Service Status Outages to campus services are listed at berkeley.statusdashboar
